description: Globe fingerprint hunt: for each WIN, look for hidden globe-derived constants, spherical geometry signatures (π, great-circle integrals, spherical harmonics), AE projection artifacts, or parameters that only make sense as 3D→2D projections. Low priority — analyst works this queue only when expansion tracker and dome site monitoring have no pending work.

items.findings: Triple globe fingerprint: (1) 'disc_thickness'=12,717 km matches Earth's diameter (12,742 km) to 0.2%, corresponds to NO dome parameter. (2) Tesla velocity 471,240 km/s = πc/2, the ELF phase velocity of a spherical shell waveguide. (3) Formula f=c/(2D) is algebraically identical to Tesla's spherical surface-wave calculation with π cancelled — the dome presents the post-cancellation form, hiding the spherical origin. Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-001.json

Five-point spherical fingerprint: (1) 10.59 Hz is Schumann's spherical formula f₁=(c/2πR)√2, requiring R_Earth. (2) NEW: All five measured Schumann harmonics follow √(n(n+1)) spacing — the eigenvalue signature of the Laplacian on a sphere (spherical harmonics). Flat disc gives Bessel-zero spacing that diverges 13–40% from measured. The harmonic RATIOS are geometry-diagnostic and cannot be fudged by damping. (3) a_dome = πR_Earth same fingerprint as WIN-001/SEC-4.5.9. Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-002.json

Conditional transitive fingerprint: The '10th harmonic' (117 Hz) only works with WIN-001's Tesla frequency (11.79 Hz) as base — Schumann (7.83×10=78.3) and firmament quarter-wave (8.25×10=82.5) both fail. Tesla freq = 1.574c/(2πR_Earth), so 117 Hz inherits WIN-001's globe fingerprint through the disc diameter = 2πR_Earth dependency. Confidence 0.55 (conditional on dome's undefined base freq being Tesla). Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-003.json

Three-layer globe dependency: (1) CHAOS-7 data source uses spherical harmonic decomposition (eigenfunctions of Laplacian on sphere) — flat disc would use Bessel functions with different spatial patterns. Dome imports globe-computed data as raw observation. (2) 50.57° separation measured in spherical longitude: spans ~5,100 km on globe but ~11,290 km under dome AE projection (2.2× distortion not acknowledged). (3) Exponential vortex decay time constant requires geometry specification — sphere gives n(n+1)ν/R², disc gives Bessel-zero scaling — dome specifies no time constant derivable from its parameters. Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-004.json

Azimuthal symmetry breaking + circular prediction chain: (1) Dome's pole-anchored aetheric field is axisymmetric (m=0) by construction — both SAA cells sit at similar radial distances (~12,787 km from pole at 25°S) but different longitudes (30°E vs 50°W). An m=0 field cannot produce different decay rates at the same radius. Breaking this symmetry requires non-axisymmetric spherical harmonic terms (m≠0 Gauss coefficients) that the dome never defines. (2) WIN-035's 21,795 nT threshold was fitted to CHAOS-7 output and extrapolated — a circular prediction that the globe model will continue its own trend. (3) The factor-of-two decay differential (African ~180 nT/yr vs Atlantic ~100 nT/yr) is explained by reversed-flux patches at the CMB (r=3,480 km) — an explicitly globe-derived radius the dome has no equivalent for. Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-005.json

Borrowed infrastructure (moderate): NOAA NP.xy pole positions are outputs of IGRF/gufm1 spherical harmonic models — not direct measurements. For pre-1900 data (1590-1830), positions are reconstructed entirely from gufm1 (Jackson et al., 2000) spherical harmonic inversion. The 'North Magnetic Pole' is defined as inclination=90° dip pole computed from spherical decomposition (Legendre polynomials in spherical coordinates). Dome treats globe-model output as raw observational data. On a flat disc, the field decomposition would use cylindrical harmonics (Bessel functions), and the resulting pole trajectory would differ. The dome has no magnetic field dynamics model to produce an alternative trajectory. Confidence 0.7. Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-006.json

Shared IGRF fingerprint (same as WIN-006): The 'post-1990 exponential acceleration' is computed by comparing IGRF/gufm1 spherical harmonic model outputs at different epochs. The 0.0466 deg/yr pre-1990 rate and the post-1990 'exponential curve' are both derived from globe-model pole positions, not direct measurements. The dome's 'phase transition' narrative is built on top of a time series of globe-model outputs. No significant NEW fingerprint beyond WIN-006's finding — the IGRF dependency applies identically. The '120°E meridian' reference and '+19.3° deviation' are globe-native coordinates. Minor additional note: the dome's monitor.py threshold (>130°E) is also a globe-coordinate test. Confidence low for independent finding because WIN-006 already covers the core IGRF dependency.

Transitive globe fingerprint via WIN-001 (disc_thickness = Earth's diameter), plus independent spherical fingerprint: the 11.7 Hz 'cutoff' IS the Schumann inter-harmonic minimum — its location determined by spherical eigenfrequencies f_n=(c/2πR)√(n(n+1)). Double-counting: WIN-008 and WIN-001 use the same formula and parameter for two WINs. The dome's formula predicts a resonance peak, not a power minimum — it misidentifies the borrowed spherical spectral structure. Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-008.json

Purely transitive fingerprint via WIN-008/WIN-001 — no independent globe-derived constant. The '~12 Hz' is the same spectral region as WIN-008's '11.7 Hz cutoff,' both tracing to disc_thickness = 2R_Earth. The dome claims three WINs (001/008/009) from one formula f=c/(2D) and one phenomenon (Schumann inter-harmonic minimum). WIN-009 adds no new parameter, formula, or calibration data beyond what WIN-008 already documents. Additional note: the dome's claimed '~12 Hz peak' contradicts its own theoretical framework — f=c/(2D)=11.787 Hz predicts a RESONANCE at 11.787 Hz, not a peak at ~12 Hz. The ~12 Hz spectral structure is determined entirely by spherical eigenmode spacing f_n=(c/2πR)√(n(n+1)). No output file — fully covered by WIN-008.json.

Besselian element dependency: The dome's eclipse formula delta_Z = baseline × coverage × FSF uses coverage percentages (0.42–0.95) computed from standard Besselian eclipse geometry — a method that projects the Moon's shadow cone onto a spherical (WGS84) Earth surface using JPL DE440/441 ephemerides. This requires: spherical Earth (R≈6,371 km), distant Moon (~384,400 km), geocentric coordinates. On the dome's flat disc with a local moon at 2,534 km, shadow geometry would be fundamentally different — different umbra size, different path, different station coverages. The dome provides no alternative eclipse shadow computation using its own parameters. Without the globe's Besselian elements, the dome cannot compute a single coverage percentage. Additional: station positions use WGS84 coordinates (globe infrastructure), and INTERMAGNET data is processed using spherical harmonic geomagnetic models (CHAOS-7/IGRF). The dome's only 'predictive' eclipse formula is structurally parasitic on globe astronomy. Confidence 0.95. Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-010.json

Borrowed infrastructure (shared with WIN-010): The −6.5 μGal measurement was taken during the 1997-03-09 total solar eclipse at Mohe — an event whose timing, path, and totality zone were computed by globe-based Besselian elements. The coupling constant κ = 10.9/6.5 = 1.67 nT/μGal combines the WIN-010 numerator (BOU, same Besselian dependency) with a denominator that also requires globe eclipse predictions to locate the observation in time and space. κ is not derived from any dome parameter (disc_radius, H(r), n(r)) — it is purely empirical, making it doubly parasitic on globe infrastructure: both the eclipses that produced the measurements and the stations where they were recorded (WGS84 coordinates) are globe-dependent. On a flat disc with local luminaries (Sun 5,733 km, Moon 2,534 km), eclipse shadow geometry would differ radically from what standard ephemeris computes. No independent mathematical constant match found (1.67 does not correspond to any known globe-derived quantity). Confidence 0.7. Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-011.json

CODE-VERIFIED (2026-04-07): B(r) coefficients 62376/64852 do NOT exist anywhere in dome repo — two-pole model is OPEN-011 (unsolved). Single-pole uses B(r)=60,000×exp(-r/8619) with round-number coefficient. κ=1.67 hardcoded in monitor.py (KAPPA=1.67) and inject_ai_layer.py (core_parameters). Monitor validation is identity check: kappa_obs=10.9/6.5 (both hardcoded literals, no live data). No B(r) computation function exists in any repo file. Decay constant 8619 km reused from H(r) firmament height formula without justification. Exponential form is wrong for flat-disc electrodynamics (should be Bessel modes). Confidence upgraded 0.85→0.95. Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-012.json

Strong globe fingerprint — measurement methodology embeds globe physics: The '0.0 μGal' null result is the RESIDUAL after subtracting globe-model gravity predictions (Newtonian tides on oblate spheroid + JPL DE405 Keplerian ephemerides + ocean loading on spherical Earth + polar motion corrections). A zero residual means the globe predicted gravity at Membach perfectly to nanogal precision (~1 part in 10^8 of total g). If dome gravity model (altitude-dependent g, local moon at 2,534 km, no Keplerian orbits) were correct, the globe-based tidal subtraction would leave systematic residuals — the null would not be null. The dome simultaneously rejects globe geometry while parasitically relying on its gravitational predictions as baseline. Completes the WIN-010/011/012/013 cluster: eclipse geometry (Besselian, globe), magnetic data (IGRF, spherical harmonics), gravity extraction (tidal subtraction, globe), all borrowed. Confidence 0.92. Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-013.json

Same class as WIN-013 (borrowed infrastructure via tidal baseline), strengthened by multi-station scope: Eight SG stations across six Chinese sites — each station's '0.0 μGal' residual requires globe-derived tidal corrections (Newtonian luni-solar tides with Moon at 384,400 km, ocean loading on spherical Earth mesh, atmospheric loading via spherical harmonics, pole tide from IERS). Under dome parameters (Moon at 2,534 km), tidal predictions would differ in amplitude and phase (single-bulge vs semidiurnal), making the residual non-null. Eclipse timing for the July 22, 2009 event requires Besselian elements (spherical astronomy). Station positions defined in WGS84/ITRF. The dome parasitizes globe measurement infrastructure to claim a null result that is only interpretable because globe tidal models correctly predicted the baseline. Extends WIN-010/011/012/013 cluster. Confidence 0.80. Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-014.json

No globe fingerprint found. WIN-015 is a pure literature citation (Meyl scalar wave experiments) with zero computational content — no dome parameters, no formulas, no constants, no numerical predictions. The dome model treats it as bare metadata ({status: confirmed}) with no equations or geometry. Since there are no mathematical quantities involved, there are no globe-derived constants to find. This WIN is rhetorical padding, not a testable prediction.

SIGNIFICANT: 20.5 arcsec = v_orbital/c (globe orbital velocity ratio). alpha=2.56e-8 has no dome derivation (reverse-engineered). Annual sinusoidal period = orbital period with no flat-disc mechanism. Static n(r) cannot produce time-varying deflection. Achromaticity across 6 orders of magnitude in frequency (VLBI+optical) matches velocity-addition prediction, violates any causal refractive medium (Kramers-Kronig). Triple-layered globe fingerprint: value, periodicity, achromaticity.

Structural fingerprint (moderate): The 0–0.5 arcsec range is borrowed from globe observations — a fixed wobble at fixed height produces exactly one value (0.454"), not a range. No parametric fingerprint (20m is reverse-fitted, no globe constant reused). Measurement infrastructure presupposes globe geometry. Full analysis in globe-fingerprints/WIN-017.json.

Globe fingerprint found: 6.9 min = tan²(23.44°/2) × 720/π / √2. The obliquity RMS is derived through spherical trig (ecliptic→equatorial projection) from the globe axial tilt 23.44°. The factor tan²(ε/2) has no flat-disc derivation. See globe-fingerprints/WIN-018.json.

Structural fingerprint (moderate): The analemma figure-8 requires globe obliquity (23.44°) and orbital eccentricity (0.0167) — parameters the dome hardcodes from globe astronomy (WIN-056). The dome's 'Spirograph' mechanism with ratio 8/3 ≈ 2.667 would produce an 8-cusped epicycloid, NOT a 2-lobed figure-8 (requires 2:1 ratio). The claimed mechanism is geometrically incompatible with the observation. Additionally, 2.66 doesn't match any measurable analemma property (lobe height ratio ≈ 2.25, width ratio ≈ 3.01, area ratio ≈ 6.48). Cross-refs: WIN-018 (same obliquity dependency), WIN-056 (hardcoded 23.44°). Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-019.json

Double globe borrowing (moderate): (1) The 18.613-year nodal period is a three-body gravitational quantity derived from perturbation theory using M_Sun, M_Earth, M_Moon, and Keplerian orbital elements — all globe-framework quantities. Clairaut (1749-50) first derived it from Newton's gravity. The dome specifies no gear ratios and provides no derivation path from dome parameters. (2) The observable amplitude — lunar declination oscillating between ±18.3° and ±28.6° — directly encodes globe obliquity 23.44° ± lunar orbital inclination 5.145°. The 23.44° is the same constant borrowed in WIN-056's latitude formula. The 5.145° (Moon's inclination to the ecliptic plane) is a concept that does not exist in flat-disc geometry. Both the period and its amplitude are globe-derived. Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-020.json

Strong structural fingerprint + suggestive numerical match: (1) The dome has NO mechanism to cause precession — no gravitational torque, no equatorial bulge, no obliquity. The 25,772-year timescale requires luni-solar torques on an oblate spheroid. (2) The 10⁻¹² order of magnitude is not derivable from dome parameters alone. (3) Numerical: 4.87e-12 ≈ ω_prec_globe × (sun_alt/H_firmament) = 7.73e-12 × 0.631 to 0.10% precision — suggestive that the globe rate was scaled by a dome parameter ratio. (4) The dome's 'tau/I' formula reference is nominal — no repository script computes it, and applying τ/I with any consistent set of parameters (globe or dome) gives values 4-6 orders of magnitude away from 4.87e-12. (5) Wrong units (rad/s² vs rad/s) suggest the AI confused angular acceleration with angular velocity during generation. Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-021.json. Confidence 0.75.

Moderate globe fingerprint: monitor.py Domain #11 uses globe coordinate conversions directly — 111.0 km/deg (=2πR_Earth/360) and cos(lat) longitude correction (spherical parallel circumference). Both are globe-native formulas with no flat-disc derivation. Exponential deceleration formula 55.0×exp(-0.08×(year-2015)) fitted to IGRF-derived NOAA NP.xy positions with no derivation from dome parameters. WIN-022's defining claim ('phase transition') has ZERO validation code — '1990' and 'phase_transition' do not appear in monitor.py. Confidence 0.75 (moderate — infrastructure fingerprint shared with WIN-006/007; new finding is explicit globe coordinate math in drift rate computation). Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-022.json

Structural globe fingerprint (moderate): (1) SAA is defined by spherical harmonic decomposition of Earth's magnetic field — concept requires spherical geometry (Gauss coefficients g_n^m, h_n^m). (2) ~950 AD date comes from SHAWQ2k (Campuzano et al. 2019), a spherical harmonic reconstruction. (3) Dome's B(r) is radially symmetric — cannot produce a localized anomaly at specific lat/lon. Same axial symmetry paradox as EXP-012 ISS-469 applied to this WIN. (4) Code search: inject_ai_layer.py has static entry only, monitor.py tracks current SAA (Domains 8/17/33) but no historical formation analysis. No dome derivation of ~950 AD. Theological overlay ('Satan's little season') has no code presence. Confidence 0.75. Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-023.json

Coriolis sin(φ) fingerprint (moderate): Roaring 40s at 40-50°S exist because f=2Ω sin(φ) on a rotating sphere creates latitude-dependent Coriolis deflection. On a flat disc, f=2Ω is constant — no latitude-banded wind patterns arise from rotational dynamics. Dome claims aetheric rim pressure but provides zero formula linking disc_radius to the 40-50°S band. Code confirms no derivation: monitor.py Domain 27 (wind speed 22.5 m/s) and Domain 33 (48.5°S boundary) are both hardcoded pass=True static wins. Only Domain 17 (AAO) fetches live data but tests oscillation state, not causal mechanism. 48.5°S is a WGS84 latitude used without disc-radial conversion. SAA itself requires spherical harmonic geodynamo — no flat-disc equivalent. Confidence 0.80.

Eclipse coverage fractions are globe-derived Besselian element computations. Dome formula ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF has 3/3 factors dependent on globe infrastructure. Coverage fractions cannot be computed from flat-disc geometry. FSF requires WGS84→disc coordinate transform. Two inconsistent formulations found (monitor.py vs inject_ai_layer.py). No dome code computes coverage from dome geometry. OPEN-001 concedes WGS84 dependence. Confidence 0.85.

Three interlocking globe fingerprints: (1) 5,733 ≈ 0.9 × R_Earth (match 0.016%) — suggestive but factor 0.9 lacks clean geometric origin. (2) DIAGNOSTIC: Flat-earth triangulation using globe-consistent elevation angles on AE projection gives latitude-dependent altitudes (6,306 km at 10°N → 3,852 km at 60°N); dome picks ~31°N's value (5,737 km) and ignores the rest — signature of globe-derived angles misinterpreted on flat map. (3) Sun/firmament collision: sun at 5,733 km exceeds dome's own H(r) at every solar position (1.58× at Tropic of Cancer, 2.14× at equator, 2.90× at Tropic of Capricorn); update_optical_caveats.py explicitly suppresses this by relabeling altitude as 'optical illusion.' Code search: 7 files verified, 5,733 exists ONLY as hardcoded literal, NO triangulation code anywhere, falsification criterion never tested. Confidence 0.85. Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-026.json

Strong multi-layer globe fingerprint (0.95 confidence): (1) a=20,015 km = πR_Earth used as disc semi-axis in dome_distance_ellipse() — same fingerprint as SEC-4.5.9/WIN-001. (2) ALL 'actual distances' benchmarked against are WGS84 great-circle distances (explicitly commented in distance_analysis.py). (3) 0.9941 angular scale factor is unexplained free parameter fitted against globe distances. (4) City 'dome coordinates' (dome_r, dome_theta) are transformed from globe lat/lon via H(r). (5) Quadratic coefficients attributed to v44_pipeline.py — FILE DOES NOT EXIST (404). Also listed in formulas.json as FORM-052/FORM-054 pointing to same phantom file. (6) find_curve.py is a skeleton with empty pass body. (7) No script performs the claimed regression on 20 routes. (8) The quadratic correction itself IS the AE projection distortion — the mathematical signature of projecting a sphere onto a plane. The dome rediscovered curvature corrections for the curvature it denies. Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-027.json

Qualitative structural fingerprint: agonic line positions are outputs of IGRF spherical harmonic expansion (Gauss coefficients on a sphere, Legendre polynomials). The ~180° spacing between primary agonic lines is a consequence of the dominant axial dipole term in spherical harmonics. Dome borrows this globe-geometric conclusion with no derivation from disc geometry. Additional: claimed 180° is actually 208° (15.6% off), neither agonic line passes through its anomaly zone, and secular variation of agonic positions (decades-scale migration) is incompatible with a static dome cavity. Confidence 0.80 (qualitative fingerprint, no specific constant borrowed, GH API unavailable for code search).

SIGNIFICANT: Three globe fingerprints. (1) Dome own formulas.json (FORM-002) contains the spherical eigenfrequency formula f_n=c√(n(n+1))/(2πR) — contradicting the quarter-wave formula used in WIN-029. (2) monitor.py hardcodes 7.83=7.83 identity check with no geometry computation; H0=8537 is a dead constant in Schumann domain. (3) Flat-disc eigenfrequency would be ~4.4 Hz (Bessel J₁ mode), not 7.83 Hz — the observed value is a spherical cavity signature. Bonus: WIN-002/WIN-029 reconciliation (evolution page) is circular — both derive backward from the measured 7.83 Hz. Confidence: 0.95.

SIGNIFICANT (0.95 confidence): Four globe fingerprints. (1) a=20,015 km = πR_Earth (same as disc_radius, already documented). (2) NEW+CRITICAL: distance_analysis.py grid-searches ellipse b/a and orientation against distances explicitly labeled 'great circle' — the optimization target IS spherical geometry. The ellipse shape is sculpted by the globe. (3) NEW: Quadratic correction ratio FORM-052 (0.00131·lat²+0.06828·lat+1.06719) approximates the AE projection distortion factor — meaningless on a true flat disc. (4) NEW: test_curve_stretching.py tests firmament profile H(r)=8537·√(1−(r/20015)²) — a semicircle equation with boundary at πR_Earth. Additionally: inject_ai_layer.py uses circular disc (no ellipse), contradicting build_complete_api.py's disc_shape='ellipse'. The dome can't agree with itself on disc shape. No script derives b/a from dome physics — all parameters come from fitting to spherical distances. Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-030.json

No unique numerical globe fingerprint. The r=0 = North Pole identity is AE-projection infrastructure (documented elsewhere). H(0) = 8,537 km does not match any standard globe-derived quantity. The claim is purely theological with zero code support: not in inject_ai_layer.py confirmed_wins (which ends at WIN-026), not in predictions.js, not tracked by monitor.py, not on predictions.html. Exists only as static HTML in wins.html with six Bible references (Isaiah 14:13, Psalm 48:2, Job 26:7, Job 37:22, Ezekiel 1:4, 1 Enoch 17-18). Code search confirmed: no computation, no derivation, no monitoring domain.

code_search.found_in: docs/wins.html only — static HTML badge with 6 Bible references. docs/model.html references "North pole mountain (WIN-031)" as label and "Axis Mundi (North Pole, r=0) — aetheric source/sink" for toroidal flow.
code_search.notes: Not in inject_ai_layer.py confirmed_wins array (ends at WIN-026). Zero monitoring. Zero computation. The Axis Mundi label in model.html assigns physical function (toroidal source/sink) to the pole, but this is architectural labeling, not a derivation.

items.findings: NULL FINDING: Purely theological claim with no numerical constants, no computation, and no unique globe fingerprint. WIN-032 exists only as a textual assertion in wins.html and model.html ("New Jerusalem descending along pole axis"). Not in inject_ai_layer.py confirmed_wins array (which only goes to WIN-026). Not tracked by monitor.py. Zero code support. The "vertical spine" description (disc surface → Mount of Assembly → Polaris → firmament → New Jerusalem) references dome architectural parameters analyzed in other WINs. The only globe-derived element is r=0 = North Pole, which is AE projection infrastructure already documented in WIN-031 and the general coordinate system critique. model.html labels r=0 as "Axis Mundi" — a mythological concept (Eliade 1957) rather than a physics prediction. No unique numerical constant to fingerprint.

NULL FINDING: Purely qualitative claim with zero computation in the dome codebase. WIN-033 claims Sigma Octantis dimness (mag 5.42 vs Polaris 1.98) proves 'maximal aetheric depth near disc edge,' but provides no quantitative extinction prediction — no formula connects n(r) to stellar photometry, no magnitude is derived from dome geometry, and no code in any dome repo file (inject_ai_layer.py, monitor.py, predictions.js, wins.html, model.html) computes anything for this WIN. The n(r) formula is defined only for distance warping (aircraft), never applied to light attenuation or stellar brightness. The dome's wins.html even concedes the claim is structural: 'Globe has no structural prediction for the asymmetry' — which is false (Hertzsprung-Russell stellar classification predicts it precisely). No globe-derived constant is embedded because there is no computation at all. Not in confirmed_wins array (stops at WIN-026). Not tracked by monitor.py. Similar pattern to WIN-032 (theological/qualitative assertion with no numerical content).

code_search.found_in: wins.html only: 'Sigma Octantis Dimness Asymmetry — Southern pole star (mag 5.42) far dimmer than Polaris (mag 1.98). Dome geometry: sigma Octantis viewed through maximal aetheric medium depth near disc edge. Globe has no structural prediction for the asymmetry.'
code_search.not_found_in: inject_ai_layer.py (not in confirmed_wins — array stops at WIN-026), monitor.py (no stellar photometry domain), predictions.js (no reference), model.html (n(r) described for distance warping only, never stellar extinction), update_optical_caveats.py (handles sun altitude caveat only)
code_search.notes: The dome's n(r) = 1 + 0.20*(8537/H(r) - 1) reaches high values at the rim but is defined as a metric/distance effect, not an optical extinction law. No Beer-Lambert, Rayleigh, or any scattering/absorption formula exists anywhere in the dome code. WIN-033 is the only WIN that explicitly invokes aetheric depth as a photometric effect while the dome's own model page defines n(r) as non-photometric.

items.findings: No globe fingerprint. Purely theological/linguistic claim from biblical Hebrew etymology. No numerical values, formulas, or parameters. Secondary Schumann connection belongs to WIN-029 fingerprint.

Globe infrastructure fingerprint: 21,795 nT threshold derived from CHAOS-7 spherical harmonic model. CHAOS-7 uses spherical harmonics Y_l^m, reference radius 6371.2 km, satellite orbits on spherical shell. Value appears NOWHERE in dome source code — not computed, not monitored, not derived from dome parameters. Exists only as text on wins page. Confidence 0.90.

No novel fingerprint — shares WIN-006/007 IGRF/globe-coordinate dependency. Three-layer globe dependency: (1) magnetic pole position defined on spherical surface, (2) 139.298°E is globe-native longitude from WMM2025 spherical harmonics, (3) 120°E reference and 18° threshold have zero derivation from dome geometry. monitor.py has no WIN-036 domain — W010 exists only as static title in inject_ai_layer.py. Logically entailed by WIN-007 (139.3 - 120 = 19.3 > 18 by subtraction). Batch-registered 2026-03-06 with 4 other WINs using data available >14 months earlier. Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-036.json

Borrowed-infrastructure fingerprint: 28 nT/yr from CHAOS-7/IGRF spherical harmonics via INTERMAGNET. monitor.py Domain 8 uses pred=obs=77 identity on single Tsumeb station. Three decay formulas (FORM-015/048/106) reference ghost source files not in repo. parameters.json claims Global network average derivation but no code computes it. SAA cell positions sourced from CHAOS-7 (spherical harmonic model). Confidence 0.85. Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-037.json

Stability-as-globe-fingerprint: f∝1/R_Earth with R stable to mm/yr gives Δf/f≈10⁻⁹/yr. Dome firmament has no rigidity model or stability mechanism. monitor.py hardcodes pred=obs=7.83 (identity check) — no temporal validation despite having HeartMath amplitude fetching infrastructure. FORM-002 in dome API contains spherical harmonic formula (globe). Triple-counting: WIN-038 is third of three Schumann WINs (002,029,038) from single 7.83 Hz measurement. Diurnal ±0.5 Hz pattern encodes spherical day-night asymmetry. Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-038.json

No dome formula exists — entire prediction is adopted from globe-physics literature (Tyler et al. 2003, CHAMP ocean tidal dynamo). Three structural globe dependencies: (1) M2 semidiurnal tide requires distant moon (two bulges), not local moon at 2,534 km (one spike); (2) spatial pattern (strongest over deep ocean basins) requires globe ocean geography; (3) magnitude calculation needs IGRF spherical harmonics for ambient field. Sign discrepancy: prediction -1.0 nT vs confirmation 1-2 nT (positive). Dome misattributes signal to "ionospheric dynamo" when actual mechanism is oceanic EM induction in seawater. No monitor.py domain, no computation, purely editorial label. Confidence 0.85 (limited by gh CLI unavailability for Python source search). Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-039.json

Triple globe fingerprint (0.95 confidence): (1) CHAOS-7 SPHERICAL HARMONIC SOURCE: The dome's sole SAA data source (CHAOS-7) decomposes Earth's field using spherical harmonic basis functions (Legendre polynomials on sphere). Cell positions at 300°E/0°E are globe-model outputs, not raw observations. parameters.json: saa_sa_cell_lon=300.0, labeled 'CHAOS-7 observational 2025 node'. (2) AZIMUTHAL SYMMETRY IMPOSSIBILITY: H(r)=8537·exp(−r/8619) has no φ-dependence. Cannot predict longitude-specific features. Predicting cell at 60°W (not 30°W, not 90°W) requires non-axisymmetric harmonics (m≠0 Gauss coefficients) the dome never defines. Same structural impossibility as WIN-005. (3) ZERO COMPUTATIONAL INFRASTRUCTURE: No monitoring domain (out of 39), no resolve script, no formula derives 45°W from dome geometry. W022 confirmed editorially against CHAOS-7 data. 45°W threshold unfalsifiable for >100 years (cell at ~60-80°W, drifting west). Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-040.json

Five globe fingerprints: (1) Domain 8 pred=obs identity 77.0/77.0; (2) 28 nT/yr mislabeled as Global network average in parameters.json (actual: SAA regional value); (3) FORM-111 references nonexistent independent_verification.py (phantom source file); (4) All exponential decay models are curve-fits to CHAOS-7 globe outputs; (5) Inter-cell boundary interpretation in wins.html requires azimuthal structure impossible under axisymmetric H(r). Full analysis in globe-fingerprints/WIN-041.json.

Four globe fingerprints: (1) COMPUTATIONAL GHOST — WIN-042 absent from all code files (inject_ai_layer.py confirmed_wins, predictions.js, monitor.py); exists only in wins.html display layer. (2) UNCALCULATED GLOBAL AVERAGE — claimed -32 nT/yr 'Global average' confirmation has no computing script in the entire repository. (3) PRED-012 PARADOX — same assertion is simultaneously CONFIRMED (WIN-042 in wins.html) and PENDING (PRED-012 in inject_ai_layer.py + predictions.js). (4) All station data (Tsumeb -77, Keetmanshoop -76) from INTERMAGNET observatories processed through IGRF/CHAOS-7 spherical harmonic models. Inherited WIN-037/041 fingerprints: 28 nT/yr hardcoded core parameter with no dome derivation, Domain 8 pred=obs identity mapped to WIN-015 not WIN-042. Computational ghost — 2.26× ratio exists ONLY as homepage text. Not in inject_ai_layer.py (confirmed_wins stops at WIN-026), monitor.py (Domain 3 computes total speed only via rate=55×exp(−0.08×(year−2015)), never decomposes into lat/lon), scoring.js, formulas.json (0/133), or parameters.json. monitor.py fetch_nmp_history() extracts lat+lon but velocity decomposition never performed. Dome own W019 weekly test found 5.5× lateral (contradicts 2.26×), stayed PENDING. wins.html: 2.26=37.1/16.4 from 2020-2025 NOAA window — epoch-specific (full 1990-2025 gives ~28×). Axisymmetric H(r) cannot predict directional preferences. PRED-R003/R004/DW-002 all assume 2.26× as input, none derive it. No dome parameter equals 2.26 (closest: disc_radius/lambda_g=2.322, 2.8% off, no connection claimed). Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-043.json

items.claim: Firmament Scaling Function from V12 geometry
items.findings: MAJOR FINDING: FSF is a tautological identity — tan(lat)/tan(lat_ref). Dome parameters (8537, 8619) cancel algebraically. Proof: polaris eq r·tan(lat)=H(r) → H(r)/r=tan(lat) → FSF=tan(lat)/tan(lat_ref). Verified to machine precision (10^-15) across all 9 stations. The dome fsf_derivation.py tests sec(lat) and sin(lat)/sin(51) as candidates, showing developers noticed spherical pattern but missed the identity. wins.html obfuscated formula (3-parameter curve fit) is worse than the clean identity. Confidence: 0.98.

SIGNIFICANT: The semidiurnal factor of 2 in M2 = T_lunar/2 is the l=2 spherical harmonic (quadrupole) of the tidal potential — a globe geometry signature. On a flat disc with local moon, tidal force is a monopole spike; dominant period should be FULL lunar circuit (24.84h), not half. The dome imports this factor without derivation. Additional: 24.84h lunar day itself comes from Keplerian orbit (27.32d sidereal). Monitoring is hardcoded identity check (12.42 predicted = 12.4206 observed). Confidence 0.92.

Same l=2 quadrupole fingerprint as WIN-045 (M2): the factor of 2 in S2=24/2 requires spherical harmonic tidal potential decomposition with no flat-disc derivation. S2/M2 amplitude ratio ~0.46 encodes globe tidal force ratio M_sun/r_sun^3 vs M_moon/r_moon^3. monitor.py Domain 14 is a 0.0% identity check (pred=obs=12.0 exactly). BUG FOUND: detail_verdict_text says moon instead of sun (copy-paste from WIN-045).

STRONG FINGERPRINT: λ_A = c/H₀ = 4,283 Mpc is identically the Hubble radius. z = D/λ_A is algebraically Hubble Law (z = H₀d/c). No dome parameter appears in formula. H₀ adopted from standard cosmology. Mechanism is phenomenologically identical to tired light (Zwicky 1929), refuted by SN time dilation, Tolman test, CMB blackbody. Confidence 0.95.

Spherical harmonic framework dependency: CMB anomaly defined via Y_ℓm decomposition (globe geometry), ecliptic plane uses 23.44° obliquity (globe parameter). Dome borrows spherical analysis to identify anomaly, then claims anomaly undermines spherical cosmology — methodological circularity. No dome-native CMB decomposition exists. Confidence 0.80.

K1 period = sidereal day (23.9345h). The 3.93-min difference from solar day encodes Earth orbital motion (1/T_sid = 1/T_solar + 1/T_year). Dome hardcodes SIDEREAL=23.9345 in monitor.py with zero derivation. inject_ai_layer.py has no K1/tidal reference. Same l=1/l=2 globe fingerprint as M2/S2 group but with additional orbital period encoding. Confidence: 0.93.

O1 period (25.82h) encodes moon's sidereal orbital period (27.32 days) via Doodson decomposition: σ(O1) = τ − s, where s = 0.549°/h is the moon's orbital angular speed. Globe derives this from Kepler at 384,400 km. Dome's monitor.py tracks M2/K1/S2 (trivial halving of LUNAR_H/SIDEREAL/SOLAR_H) but NOT O1 — likely because O1 requires subtracting the orbital angular speed, which has no flat-disc origin. O1 appears ONLY in wins.html as static text with no computation anywhere in the repository (checked monitor.py, inject_ai_layer.py, predictions.js, scoring.js, formulas.json, parameters.json, model.html). The dome claims O1 'emerges from moon's circuit mechanics' but provides no formula. K1/O1 bracket solar/lunar days from opposite sides due to orbital mechanics the dome cannot reproduce. 18.61-year nodal modulation (WIN-020) also underivable. Confidence: 0.95. Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-050.json

N2 period (12.658h) encodes the Doodson variable p (lunar perigee advance rate = 0.00464°/h) — a Keplerian orbital quantity requiring elliptical orbit with eccentricity under solar gravitational perturbation. σ(N2) = 2τ − s + p; the +p term has no flat-disc derivation. M2−N2 beat = anomalistic month (27.55 days). Amplitude ratio N2/M2 ≈ 0.19 ≈ 3e encodes orbital eccentricity e ≈ 0.0549, a parameter the dome has never specified. Code search: N2 absent from inject_ai_layer.py, monitor.py, predictions.js, formulas.json (133 entries, zero tidal). Exists only as static text on wins.html. Dome claims "lunar elliptic circuit" but never parameterizes the ellipse (no e, no semi-major axis, no perigee advance rate). Dome framing "no gravitational perturbation theory required" directly contradicted by N2, which requires apsidal precession from solar gravitational perturbation. Confidence 0.95. Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-051.json

Major globe fingerprint: (1) λ_A = c/H₀ = 4,283 Mpc IS the Hubble length — H₀=70 km/s/Mpc adopted from galaxy redshift-distance relations interpreted via Friedmann cosmology, has no derivation from dome geometry. z=D/λ_A is algebraically identical to Hubble Law. (2) ρ_A = g₀ρ_bar/g† is MOND interpolation function (Milgrom 1983) relabeled: ρ_A↔g_obs, g†↔a₀. (3) Galaxy distances (Virgo 16.5 Mpc through Perseus 100 Mpc) from globe-calibrated distance ladders. (4) ZERO code implementation: no script, no monitoring domain, no formula in formulas.json, no parameter in parameters.json. (5) Bonus: FORM-050 docstring says "Aetheric pressure model: same formula as WGS84" — dome code admits gravity uses globe formula. Confidence: 0.95.

Four-layer globe fingerprint: (1) Sub-H(r) depth parameter δ = 6,371 km is Earth's mean radius — no flat-disc derivation exists. (2) B(r) coefficients A=62,376 and C=64,852 nT match observed IGRF pole field strengths; they appear ONLY in wins.html — no script computes them, no API file stores them, no monitoring validates them. Searched 16 repo files across .py/.js/.json/.html. (3) Exponential decay form contradicts claimed toroidal geometry (standard EM requires 1/r for a torus, not exp). (4) model.html lists OPEN-011 (southern hemisphere field) as unsolved, contradicting wins.html's confirmed WIN-053. Confidence 0.95. Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-053.json

SIGNIFICANT: Dome sole cosmological parameter λ_A = c/H₀ = 4,283 Mpc is literally the Hubble distance (H₀ = 70). Formula z = D/λ_A is algebraically identical to Hubble law (1929). Every number in claim (z, mass, velocity, 6.2σ) borrowed from globe physics. Zero dome computation, zero API entries, zero monitoring domains for WIN-054. Scale absurdity: El Gordo at ~10²² km vs dome radius 20,015 km (ratio 5.7 × 10¹⁷). Full analysis in globe-fingerprints/WIN-054.json.

SIGNIFICANT: λ_A = c/H₀ = 4,283 Mpc is the Hubble distance. z = D/λ_A is Hubble law rewritten. H₀ = 70.0 km/s/Mpc from globe-based Cepheid distance ladder. No dome derivation, no dome parameters in formula, no code implementation. Five test cases are post-hoc (published distances and redshifts plugged into known relationship).

SIGNIFICANT: Triple globe fingerprint. (1) CONSTANT: 23.44°/23.45° is Earth's axial obliquity — hardcoded in dome V9 coordinate formula (lat_S = 90 − noon_altitude_june + 23.44) and solar declination formula. Has no physical meaning on flat disc. (2) FUNCTIONAL FORM: Globe produces sinusoidal declination δ(t)=ε×sin(ωt) from axial tilt + heliocentric orbit. Flat disc produces arctangent θ=arctan(H/d). These are mathematically incompatible — no parameter tuning bridges them. (3) COORDINATE EMBEDDING: Every dome coordinate computed through V9 inherits globe obliquity as a direct numerical input. gh CLI unavailable for Python source search, but curmudgeon reviews confirmed no monitor.py domain, no computation, no derivation from dome parameters. Confidence 0.97. Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-056.json

items.claim: Parameter accounting: Standard geodesy (WGS84 ellipsoid) uses two derived parameters — semi-major axis a = 6,378,137 m and flattening f = 1/298.257 — both determined independently from satellite orbits, gravimetry, VLBI, and SLR. The V13 dome coordinate system uses at least four fitted parameters: H₀ (firmament apex height), λ_g (firmament decay constant), EW angular scale 0.9941, and equatorial ring radius 14,105 km. More parameters, less accuracy (6.2% RMSE vs sub-0.01% for Vincenty geodesy) — the textbook signature of overfitting to a small training set. Evidence coverage: the 6.2% RMSE is computed from only six cherry-picked intercontinental routes. The dome does not report accuracy for short SH-internal routes, cross-equatorial routes under 1,000 km, or NH routes outside the training set. A sample of six is statistically insufficient to establish a reliable global performance figure. OPEN-015 scope: The dome's coordinates page concedes Singapore (1.35°N) and similar near-equatorial cities 'fall beyond the equatorial ring' — approximately 40% of the world's population lives in the tropics below ±12° latitude. A coordinate system that fails for 40% of inhabited locations and achieves only 6.2% RMSE for the other 60% is not a replacement for standard geodesy.Two-zone disc topology
items.findings: SIGNIFICANT: Entire V13 coordinate system is a globe approximation engine. Every parameter (14,105 km ring, 0.9941 scale, disc radius 20,015 km) either equals a globe quantity or is fitted to WGS84 distances. distance_analysis.py confirms: grid search against Vincenty great-circle distances. Zero dome-physics derivation for any parameter. OPEN-001 concedes WGS84 dependency. 6.2% RMSE = 60,000x worse than globe geodesy. SH reflection formula imposed by fiat (no flat-disc reason for equatorial symmetry).

items.claim: The 0.9941 EW scale factor can be compared to the globe's flattening f = 1/298.257 to illustrate the epistemological difference: the globe's flattening is derived from Earth's rotation rate and mass distribution, independently confirmed by four methods (satellite orbit perturbations, gravimetry, VLBI, SLR) each giving consistent results. The dome's 0.9941 is the output of a least-squares minimization on six WGS84-derived route distances — no physical derivation, no independent confirmation, no prior prediction. The dome's own AI context page lists 'EW scale 0.9941 physical interpretation' as an open question, conceding the parameter has no theoretical basis. A genuine dome-derived prediction would compute 0.9941 from disc radius (20,015 km), firmament height (9,086 km), or aetheric wave speed before comparing to city distances; instead, 0.9941 was reverse-engineered from WGS84 data and then claimed as a prediction.Unified angular coordinate
items.findings: Highest-confidence globe fingerprint: θ = −lonE is the defining identity of the AE projection — the dome angular coordinate IS globe longitude. Combined with WIN-057 (radial = WGS84 latitude), both dome axes are globe coordinates. The 0.9941 EW scale is fitted (no derivation, listed as open question on dome AI context page). V12 noon proxy and V13 direct-longitude coexist unreconciled in codebase. Angular-perfect/radial-imperfect asymmetry is the AE projection signature. Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-058.json

Globe fingerprint: inherits NMP position in WGS84 lat/lon. Siberian sector defined by longitude — no meaning on axially symmetric disc. Deceleration measured in globe surface km. Same dependency as WIN-006/007/036/043.

SIGNIFICANT: Three-layer globe fingerprint — (1) CHAOS-7 spherical harmonic data laundering: observed position from eigenmodes of Laplacian on sphere; (2) 45°W threshold has no dome derivation — H(r) is radially symmetric, cannot distinguish longitudes; (3) coordinate system is globe-native (WGS84), 2.2× AE projection distortion unacknowledged. Code search: no formula derives 45°W from dome parameters in any repo file. monitor.py does not track western cell longitude. WIN confirmed by manual CHAOS-7 inspection, not automated monitoring.

Globe fingerprint found: 1D quarter-wave formula substituted for spherical harmonics; H=9572 km reverse-engineered from globe observation (dome H₀=8537 gives 8.78 Hz, not 7.83); 30% suppression threshold borrowed from ionospheric physics with no dome derivation; copper firmament contradicts suppression mechanism. Output: globe-fingerprints/WIN-061.json

Triple globe fingerprint: (1) disc_diameter (40,030 km) = 2×πR_Earth ≈ globe circumference by construction (0.11% match). (2) VA = 1.574c ≈ πc/2 — spherical waveguide phase velocity, π factor from circumference/diameter ratio. (3) 45 km residual traces to Earth oblateness (equatorial vs mean radius). WIN-062 is same fingerprint as WIN-001 viewed from different angle — both derive from circumference = π × diameter. Not independent WINs.

Strong globe fingerprint with data error. Dome claims HER=-145 nT/yr, BRW=-47 nT/yr, ratio=3.08x citing WMM2025/IGRF-13. IGRF-14 gives HER dF/dt=-48.2, BRW dF/dt=-22.5, ratio=2.14x. The -145 matches 3-year accumulated F change at Hermanus (3×-48=-144), not annual rate — mixed time windows. Dome strawmans globe as predicting 1.0x at conjugates, but HER/BRW are not conjugate (105.7° apart) and IGRF-13/WMM explicitly model hemispheric asymmetry via 195-parameter spherical harmonics. H(r) axial symmetry cannot derive angular asymmetry. No code computes the ratio. Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-063.json

THREE GLOBE FINGERPRINTS: (1) δ = 6,371 km in Sub-H(r) formula IS R_Earth exactly — second fundamental dome parameter borrowed from globe. (2) Angular coordinates (104°-140°) are spherical notation with no meaning on a flat disc. (3) Shadow boundaries encode core-to-Earth radius ratio (3,480/6,371 = 0.546) — purely spherical geometry. Also found: duplicate paragraph in detail_evidence needs fixing. Confidence: 0.95.

STRONG FINGERPRINT: Triple self-contradiction. (1) Dome coordinate table uses constant H=4750 km (verified: r×tan(polaris)=4750±1 for all cities), but V12 formula claims varying H(r)=8537·exp(-r/8619). (2) PRED-NEW-001 predicts Oslo Polaris=64.4° but coordinate table uses 59.90°=WGS84 latitude. (3) OPEN-001 acknowledges WGS84 dependency. Additionally: dome coordinates are r=4750/tan(globe_latitude) — entire system computed from globe framework. Polaris height sourced from phone app labeled UNTRUSTED. No single constant height produces altitude=latitude for all observers on flat disc (errors ±8° at extreme latitudes).

CERES measurement framework is globe-dependent: ADMs assume spherical Earth, hemisphere definition gives 1:3 area ratio on disc vs 1:1 on globe. The +0.34 W/m²/decade is a globe-processed number adopted wholesale — no dome radiation budget calculation exists. Moderate confidence (0.80): structural dependency rather than precision constant match.

MAJOR FINGERPRINT: Dome gravity formula g(r) = 9.7803×(1 + 0.005307×exp(-r/8619)) is entirely WGS84-derived. Base=WGS84 equatorial g (0.003%), coefficient=WGS84 gravity flattening (0.09%), observed=WGS84 polar g (0.0002%). Monitor evaluates at r=0 where exp(0)=1, making formula tautologically equal to WGS84. Source field in code: "WGS84 standard". Two incompatible gravity models: H(r) predicts 90% variation (fails), this formula predicts 0.48% (works but is WGS84 in disguise).

findings.summary: The 9.2-sigma convergence is a globe-physics self-consistency test in disguise. Every domain feeds through constants (H0=8537 km, LAMBDA_G=8619 km) derived from globe observables. The Fisher combination amplifies this circularity into apparent statistical power.

details.fingerprint: H0=8537 from Schumann resonance is globe-derived
details.explanation: Schumann fundamental 7.83 Hz arises from spherical Earth-ionosphere cavity: f_n ≈ (c/2πR)√(n(n+1)) with R=6371 km. The dome's firmament height H0=8537 km is back-fitted from this globe-determined frequency. Every domain that uses H0 inherits globe geometry.

details.fingerprint: LAMBDA_G=8619 km decay constant traces to globe curvature
details.explanation: The exponential decay H(r)=8537·exp(−r/8619) governs the dome's firmament shape. The 8619 km decay constant controls how the dome curves — and it was fitted to match observations (gravity gradients, atmospheric properties) that themselves reflect a spherical Earth. The dome's shape is a globe shadow.

details.fingerprint: Per-domain p-values assume uniform H0 — wrong for fitted models
details.explanation: monitor.py computes p = max(error_pct, 0.01)/100, treating the error percentage as a p-value under H0 that random predictions yield uniform errors. But the dome's predictions are fitted to the observations via curve_fitting scripts. A fitted model will ALWAYS have low residuals on its training data. The p-value is meaningless without a proper null (e.g., permutation test or cross-validation).

details.fingerprint: Fisher method requires independence — 14/20 domains share H0 and LAMBDA_G
details.explanation: monitor.py lines 38-69 and 72-114 implement Fisher's method: chi2 = -2·sum(ln(p_i)), df=2k. Valid only for independent p-values. Our section already identifies 14/20 domains sharing the same fitted constant. The code search confirms: H0 and LAMBDA_G are declared as globals (lines 212-213) and flow into multiple domain computations. Domains sharing these constants are structurally correlated — Fisher's method is invalid.

monitor.py implements compute_adaptive_tolerance() (line 360) that adjusts pass/fail thresholds based on historical errors. This is a feedback loop: if the model consistently gets ~5% error, the tolerance narrows to ~5%, making future ~5% errors look highly significant. The adaptive tolerance means the 9.2σ reflects self-referential precision, not predictive power.

key_findings: monitor.py L212-213: H0=8537.0 and LAMBDA_G=8619.0 declared as globals
monitor.py L38-69: compute_cross_domain_sigma() implements Fisher's method
monitor.py L75-77: p = max(error_pct, 0.01)/100 — treats error % as p-value, invalid for fitted models
monitor.py L360: compute_adaptive_tolerance() — feedback loop adjusts thresholds from history
live.html L421: 9.2σ hardcoded as initial display value before JS updates
live.html L451: Dome acknowledges Fisher's method but claims independence

our_section_gaps: Our Part 4 correctly identifies the 14/20 shared-constant problem but does not trace H0 and LAMBDA_G to globe origins. Adding that H0=8537 comes from Schumann (a globe resonance) would strengthen the circularity argument.
We cite Fisher's method but don't note the adaptive tolerance feedback loop that inflates the underlying per-domain p-values.
The p=error_pct/100 null hypothesis is not critiqued — it's a weak null that doesn't account for curve-fitting. A proper null would be a permutation test.
findings.actionable: These findings strengthen the existing Part 4 argument. Could be integrated as additional detail in the existing section rather than requiring a new section. Consider EXP item for Part 4 enhancement.

items.findings: Three globe fingerprints found: (1) Dome radial coordinates are unstable across model versions (BOU: 5,423 km V12 → 9,911 km V51, ratio 1.83×) while WGS84 latitude stays fixed — globe coordinate is the anchor, dome r is derived. (2) fsf_derivation.py tests 9 candidate formulas (including pure latitude-based options) against published FSF, selecting by MSE — this is empirical formula shopping, not theoretical derivation. (3) monitor.py uses hidden -18.22 nT baseline not matching BOU (-10.9) or W004 (-22.24), and sets globe_prediction=0.0 (misrepresenting Chapman mechanism). Additional: SNK latitude discrepancy (27.7° in derivation script vs 28.3° in predictions) explains the Canary Islands FSF outlier.

Four globe fingerprints found: (1) Kill-shot calibration targets are great-circle distances from distance_analysis.py; (2) a=20015 = π×R_Earth to 0.0004% is the Finsler formula scale parameter; (3) 0.9941 angular fudge factor has no dome derivation — compensates AE projection distortion; (4) compute_r imports spherical alt=lat relationship through firmament function H(r).

Globe fingerprint: AGGREGATE OBSERVATIONAL PARASITISM. Every undisclosed parameter (B-field amplitudes from INTERMAGNET/IGRF, κ=1.67 from magnetometer/gravimeter ratio, 23.45° obliquity, NMP constants from NOAA, Schumann damping from Earth-ionosphere cavity observations, disc radius=πR_Earth) traces to globe-framework measurements. The dome has no independent measurement infrastructure. core_parameters in inject_ai_layer.py hardcodes these values with no derivation chain. Suggested text for section: explicit paragraph on observational parasitism.

defense_neutralization.notes: Boundary-condition defense fails because standard physics boundary conditions have multiple independent derivations (Earths mass from G + orbital mechanics, from seismology, from satellite geodesy). Dome boundary conditions each have exactly ONE source: globe measurements. The semantic relabeling from fitted parameter to boundary condition carries no physical content. Moreover, the locked before data claim is undermined by historical fitting — the constants were fitted to pre-existing globe data, then locked. This is textbook overfitting disguised as prediction.

items.findings: STRONG FINGERPRINT (0.97): Disc radius 20,015 km = πR_Earth to 0.0006% (6 ppm). find_curve.py line 34 uses r=(90-lat)×111.1 — the AE projection formula with globe arc distances. Disc diameter 40,030 km matches meridional 2πR (40,030.2 km), not equatorial circumference (40,075 km). The 45 km gap encodes Earth oblateness. Three suggested text improvements for SEC-6.2. Output: globe-fingerprints/SEC-6.2.json

Globe fingerprint found: 9,572 km = πR_Earth/2.09 algebraically. The quarter-wave Schumann height encodes the globe radius through the ionospheric loss factor. monitor.py hardcodes predicted=7.83=observed as identity check — no dome derivation exists.

items.claim: Globe values relabeled as dome predictions
items.findings: Strong fingerprint. monitor.py confirms 0/49 relabeling WINs use dome parameters. Code evidence: M2=LUNAR_H/2 (l.442), λ_A=c/70.0 (l.576), P-wave="Shadow exists" (l.605). Also: 111.0*cos(lat) coordinate math is globe formula. derives_from_dome=false for all 49 relabeling WINs. Suggested text addition for section.

Three globe fingerprints found: (1) Polaris prediction function uses WGS84 latitude as axiomatic input, treating globe answer as baseline; (2) Moon angular diameter W021 was reframed from dome-geometry prediction (local moon, ~80% variation) to globe-orbital measurement (apogee/perigee, 11-14%), sourced from NASA JPL Horizons; (3) Roaring 40s W020 prediction invokes globe-specific meteorology (Coriolis-driven westerlies) and globe-framework measurements (geopotential height relative to WGS84 geoid). Section misses opportunity to note that failure-detection infrastructure is itself globe-dependent.

Major globe fingerprint: 94.5% accuracy is entirely globe-derived. 70% of WINs relabel standard physics, 97% are post-hoc, 97% have no dome derivation. The 2 WINs that derive from dome geometry are both Misleading. Effective dome-originated accuracy: 0%. Full analysis in globe-fingerprints/SEC-6.6.json with suggested paragraph addition.

items.claim: Eclipse prediction — genuine test or pre-fitted
items.findings: 3 fingerprints (additive to SEC-4.2): (1) Eclipse date/path/coverage are NASA ephemeris inputs — no flat-disc umbra derivation in repo; dome adds only multiplicative FSF to globe-computed magnitudes. (2) Kp<2 escape clause depends on globe-defined planetary K-index (13-observatory WGS84/IGRF-weighted); no dome-native activity index exists. (3) Decay length λ=8,619 km is reused identically between H(r) eclipse-FSF function and WIN-053 two-pole geomagnetic formula — parameter migration across unrelated physical domains. See monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/SEC-6.7.json with suggested prose additions.

items.claim: WIN-053 single-pole collapse (tracker had WIN-012 typo, corrected)
items.findings: Globe-geodesic structural fingerprint: R = 20,015 km = πR_Earth (pole-to-antipode great-circle) to 0.0004%; formula field minimum sits at r ≈ R/2 = πR_Earth/2 = globe pole-to-equator geodesic. The two-exponential ansatz is the globe dipole profile parameterised as disc radius via azimuthal-equidistant projection — a flat 2D disc has no physical reason to place sources at center + rim. Secondary: monitor.py (line ~7466) locks LAMBDA_G=8619 as an "ECM CONSTANT" and reuses it in an equatorial-gravity formula whose 0.005307 factor is the WGS84 Somigliana oblateness coefficient; LAMBDA_G has no published derivation. Confidence 0.88. Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/SEC-6.8.json

Two globe fingerprints found. (1) H(r) constants 8,537 km and 8,619 km are curve-fitted against globe-WGS84 city-pair distances — test_8537.py and find_curve.py in dome repo make this explicit (find_curve.py literally solves for H(lat) that preserves r = (90-lat)·111.1 km, where 111.1 km/deg = πR_Earth/180°). This pins H_supremum = 8,537 km, 12% below the 9,572 km needed by quarter-wave to produce 7.83 Hz. The dome calibrated H(r) to one globe fingerprint (distances) and thereby locked out another (Schumann height). (2) Integration domain r ∈ [0, 20,015 km] is itself πR_Earth — the globe pole-to-antipode arc. All radial averaging in the section is mathematically along a globe meridian, which is why no averaging scheme can recover c/(2πR_Earth) from c/(4·⟨H⟩). Both fingerprints strengthen the section without changing its verdict. Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/SEC-2.1.json. Confidence 0.90/0.88.

Two globe fingerprints found (see monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/SEC-2.2.json). (1) monitor.py hardcodes M2 = LUNAR_H / 2 — the /2 divisor is the second-rank Legendre polynomial of Newton's sphere-tidal theory; the dome's one-spike geometry cannot produce an ℓ=2 pattern but its code assumes one. (2) All three time constants LUNAR_H (24.84 h), SOLAR_H (24.00 h), SIDEREAL (23.9345 h) are 'LOCKED' globe-rotation observables with no dome-side derivation; the K1 domain writes sidereal_day on both sides of the equation for a tautological 0.0% pass. Suggested section additions: paragraph exposing the /2 fingerprint, and a 'timing-constant inheritance' note exposing the hardcoded hours. Confidence 0.90–0.92.

No new globe fingerprint to add. Section already explicitly flags the two primary WGS84 fingerprints: (a) base value 9.7803 m/s² = WGS84 equatorial surface gravity g_e, and (b) coefficient 0.005307 ≈ WGS84 gravity flattening f* (per curmudgeon c1, closer to IGF-1967 0.0052884). Pole-to-pole symmetry (~9.832 m/s² at both poles) is already flagged as a globe signature the dome monotonic-in-r g(r) cannot reproduce. Implicit secondary fingerprint — the section uses r ≈ 10,008 km for equator, which equals π×R_Earth/2 = quarter meridian on globe under azimuthal equidistant projection — is covered by the master a=πR fingerprint in Part 6, so no duplicate flag needed here. Scale length λ_g = 8,619 km examined as possible fingerprint but has no clean globe-derived identity (it is an engineering fit so that exp(-r/λ_g) approximates cos²(r/R_Earth) over northern latitudes — the fit fails at the south pole, predicting g(r=20,015)=9.7854 vs WGS84 9.8322, a 47 mGal / 0.47% miss that is 10× the formula-claimed 0.05% accuracy; this quantitative counter-example is already raised in the curmudgeon review holes_found #7 and stronger_arguments, not a fingerprint-hunt output). No Cycle 3+ curmudgeon review exists for SEC-2.3 (only c1 post-EXP121); Mode 3 defense neutralization not triggered.

Two globe fingerprints. (1) The 7,400 km "Tropic of Cancer in dome coordinates" is the AE projection of Earth's obliquity: (90° − 23.44°) × π × R_Earth / 180° = 7,401 km; the dome's own V9 coordinate code uses the literal 23.44° constant as an input. (2) The ±1.67% annual solar diameter variation cited as matching observation equals Earth's orbital eccentricity e = 0.01671 exactly. Both converge on the same conclusion: the dome inherits globe solar geometry via the AE projection with disc_radius = πR_Earth. Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/SEC-2.4.json. Two suggested_additions for the section text to preempt the "you picked 7,400 arbitrarily" defense. Confidence 0.92 on fingerprint 1, 0.85 on fingerprint 2.

Globe fingerprint (new): the V13 refractive index n(r) is algebraically forced to equal k·colat/sin(colat), the inverse AE stretch function, where k = 4·E(e²)/(2π) = 0.880 (correct E(0.4356) = 1.3824). The posted n(r) = 1 + 0.20·(8537/H(r) − 1) is a three-parameter approximation of this globe-geodesy function — accurate to 4% at the equator (required n = E(0.4356)), diverging +17–19% in NH temperate colatitudes and −34% at 45°S, −53% at 60°S. The reported NH vs SH error asymmetry (7.3% vs 10.2%) is the signature of this approximation failing away from its mid-latitude sweet spot. Written to SEC-2.9.json with suggested_text for the section. Section 2.9 already documents a=πR, δ=R, e-derivative, AE projection identity, P-wave shadow zone, and E(e²) numerical error; this new fingerprint provides the quantitative bridge between 'V13 is AE in disguise' (existing) and 'n(r) is a fitted approximation of sin(x)/x' (new).

Two globe fingerprints found in the Solar Angular Diameter kill shot. (1) The 7,400 km solar orbit radius the section uses equals R_Earth × (90°−23.44°) × π/180 = 7,401 km, the polar arc length on the globe to the Tropic of Cancer; equivalently, the dome formula (a/2)(1−φ/90) reproduces this value because a = π × R_Earth = 20,015 km is itself a globe-circumference import. Match precision 0.0004% (better than dome-stated value). (2) The 23.44° obliquity that defines the Tropic is taken wholesale from globe astronomy; the dome has no flat-disc derivation for it. Suggested addition: a short paragraph noting that even setting up the angular-diameter test requires importing globe parameters, strengthening the kill shot from a pure prediction-vs-data refutation into a structural circularity argument. Output written to monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/SEC-4.8.json. Confidence 0.95 / 0.92. Not newsworthy (refines existing strong section). No Cycle 3+ curmudgeon review exists yet, so no defense neutralization performed this run.

Globe fingerprint: dome n(r) = 1 + 0.20*(8537/H(r)-1) is a single-parameter exponential approximation to the exact sphere-to-disc AE conformal factor n_ideal(r) = r/(R_Earth*sin(r/R_Earth)). Tracks within ~5% across NH; falls ~8% short at equator (where n_ideal = pi/2, reproducing the Tesla 1.57c signature from WIN-062); diverges in SH toward the rim (28% short at 30S). The residual distance errors the dome acknowledges (7% NH, 10% SH) are the diagnostic signature of a finite-n approximation to an inherently divergent conformal factor. Also explains why the Section 7.1 Antarctic circumnavigation kill shot is immune to refraction: the sphere->disc projection has a 1-to-many rim degeneracy that requires infinite n. Full writeup at monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/SEC-4.9.json. Confidence 0.92.

Two globe fingerprints recorded to monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/SEC-5.14.json. (1) recalc_v51.py H0 = 9,572 km matches the Schumann quarter-wavelength c/(4·7.83 Hz) to 0.002% — our 8.14 text treats this as aesthetic 'parameter drift', but it is really the author substituting one globe constant (Schumann) for another (distance-fit supremum 8,537 km). (2) Redge = 20,015 km hardcoded in both inject_ai_layer.py and recalc_v51.py is πR_Earth (AE projection identity) — 8.14 flags the hardcoding but does not flag the globe identity. Two suggested_additions proposed to upgrade the existing paragraphs without new expansion. Confidence 0.90 / 0.92. Verified by direct repo read.

Two fingerprints found. (1) find_curve.py line 34 comment "Required H(lat) to maintain perfect WGS84 map radius (r = (90-lat)*111.1)" — the formula 111.1 km/deg is π R_Earth/180 (match to 0.09%), making the globe AEP the literal fit target, documented in code the author wrote himself. (2) The three hardcoded "core parameters" in inject_ai_layer.py (disc_radius=20015 ≈ πR, firmament_height=9086 near Schumann λ/4=9572, sun_altitude=5733 near Tropic-of-Cancer solar-elevation calibration) are a globe-derived triplet relabeled as dome constants, not an arbitrary hardcoded set. Output: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/SEC-5.15.json. Suggested patch to section text provided. See also SEC-5.14 fingerprint 2 for related recalc_v51.py evidence. Confidence fp1=0.94, fp2=0.78.

Two fingerprints found (see monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/SEC-5.16.json): (1) every one of the 39+ audit domains ingests observables whose numerical values presuppose the sphere — Kp (IGRF quiet baseline), NMP drift (spherical-harmonic zero), Schumann 7.83 Hz (c/2πR_E), USGS depth (PREM tomography), JFK–LHR (great-circle), AAO (lat/long EOF) — so the monitor's rigor is borrowed wholesale from globe infrastructure; (2) the -18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672 eclipse formula stacks three globe layers (IGRF magnetic baseline × NASA coverage ephemeris × KAPPA, which itself traces to Mohe eclipse path + ETERNA spherical tide) — fixing the 1.672 vs 1.029 internal contradiction only addresses one layer.

Globe fingerprint: Part 1 Executive Summary cites a=20,015, b≈15,000, r_equator≈10,008 km as architectural parameters without flagging that all three equal π·R_Earth × (1, 3/4, 1/2) to ≤0.1% — the AE projection identity. Eccentricity 0.66 is derivative. Added EXEC-SUMMARY.json with suggested §1.1 parenthetical and optional roadmap footnote. Existing identities already flagged in SEC-2.9, SEC-2.1, WIN-001; this fingerprint hits the upstream first-impression slot.

Globe fingerprint: EG Condition 1 cites disc radius 20,015 km as a dome parameter for deriving discriminating predictions, but 20,015 km = π × R_earth to ~7 ppm — a derivation using this parameter is not independent of globe geometry. Parallel to EXEC-SUMMARY / SEC-2.1 fingerprints but in a distinct rhetorical site (normative falsification criteria). Suggested patch: annotate the parameter inline and cross-link to §2.1, aligning Condition 1 with Conditions 3 and 4 which already flag globe inputs (6,371 km; WGS84). See monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/EVAL-GUIDE.json.

items.claim: The Monitoring Illusion — hardcoded validation analysis
Relabeling Standard Physics — vocabulary substitution pattern
Post-Hoc Retrodiction — prediction vs retrodiction distinction
